name: Grade 1 Learning Commons — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: AASL and ISTE student standards
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Learning Commons pathway.

units.title: Investigate: research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to research questions, tool choice, collaboration, and digital citizenship.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
